candidates.why: Explicit COGS-related D&A row, but it appears as a component beneath the selected total row 'Cost of revenues/COGS' and is already embedded in that total, especially given the companion row 'Expenses corresponding to products sold excl Depr/Amort'.
Generic D&A row is unpopulated in the excerpt and does not provide a separate operating amount to add.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is '[Income Statement] Cost of revenues/COGS'. The statement shows a clear decomposition into 'Depreciation/Amortization in COR/COGS' plus 'Expenses corresponding to products sold excl Depr/Amort', which indicates the selected COGS total already includes COGS D&A. Adding it separately would double count.

candidates.why: Explicit S&M D&A row, but it is presented as a component of the selected parent row 'Sales and Marketing Expenses', alongside 'Sales and marketing expenses exl Dep/Amortization'.
Explicit administration D&A row, but it appears to be included within the selected parent row 'Administration Expenses', supported by the balancing/excluding rows shown beneath it.

Explicit R&D D&A row, but it appears to be a component of the selected parent row 'Research and development expenses'; also R&D is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so adding R&D D&A separately would distort the intended XSGA minus XRD logic.
Generic D&A row is unpopulated and not a usable separate operating SG&A D&A amount.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA reference bucket is the sum of 'Sales and Marketing Expenses', 'Administration Expenses', and 'Research and development expenses'. Each selected row has a corresponding 'exl Dep/Amortization' line plus explicit D&A component row beneath it, indicating the selected totals already include those D&A amounts. Therefore no SG&A-related D&A should be added separately.
